name: Maple-Pepper Roasted Pork Loin
recipeIngredient: 1 boneless center-cut trimmed pork loin, 3 to 4 inches high (5 pounds)
Salt and pepper to season marinated meat
recipeInstructions:
1. Place pork in a plastic cooking bag.
2. Combine juice, oil, maple syrup, shallots, garlic, bay leaves, thyme, lemon zest, and pepper.
3. Add to meat in the bag. Seal the bag and refrigerate overnight or up to 24 hours for marinating.
4. Prepare glaze as directed and refrigerate until ready to use.
5. Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
6. Remove pork from marinade and discard marinade.
7. Place meat on a drain rack over a sheet pan.
8. Season with salt and pepper.
9. Place in the preheated oven and cook for 10 minutes.
10. Baste with glaze and continue cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
11. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F (175°C).
12. Cook for another 10 minutes, basting once.
13. Continue cooking, basting every 10 minutes, until the meat reaches an internal temperature of 155°F (68°C), about 20 minutes total.
14. At this point, pork should be cooked to medium well and will have a hint of pink. If desired, roast can be cooked for an additional 5 to 10 minutes.
15. Remove meat from oven and let it rest for 15 to 20 minutes before slicing. This will bring the meat's internal temperature up about 10 degrees.
16. Check the roast with a meat thermometer at this point. If the temperature has not reached 160°F (71°C), return the meat to the oven until it reaches that temperature.
17. Slice into 32 equal slices for 16 servings.
18. Serve with corn potatoes and chutney. Makes 16 servings.
Note: Roasts that are thicker and shorter in length should be cooked longer to reach an internal temperature of 155°F (68°C). Let rest about 20 minutes before slicing.
